page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.gn8is.4xlarge is a GPU-accelerated server instance designed for workloads requiring dedicated parallel processing capabilities. It is equipped with an Intel Xeon 6462C processor operating at a clock speed of 3.3 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 16 dedicated physical cores with a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio, ensuring no resource sharing at the CPU level. It is configured with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core.

Acceleration is delivered via a single NVIDIA L20 GPU based on the Ada Lovelace architecture, which includes 48 GB of dedicated VRAM. The instance does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met through network-attached block storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 16 Gbps, and the instance runs on the KVM hypervisor.

From a resource density perspective, the combination of a dedicated 16-core Intel Xeon CPU, 128.0 GB of RAM, and a 48 GB NVIDIA L20 GPU offers a balanced profile for memory-intensive and GPU-accelerated tasks. The lack of local storage represents a tradeoff, requiring external storage configuration but allowing for flexible scaling of storage resources. This hardware configuration is suitable for workloads such as machine learning inference, data science pipelines, graphics rendering, and memory-intensive analytical databases.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.gn8is.4xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance built on the KVM hypervisor and x86_64 architecture. It features 16 dedicated physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6462C processor running at 3.3 GHz, paired with 128.0 GB of system memory. Graphics and parallel computing workloads are accelerated by a single NVIDIA L20 GPU from the Ada Lovelace family, offering 48 GB of VRAM. The instance does not include local storage, relying instead on network-attached storage options, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 16 Gbps. This hardware profile is designed for memory-intensive GPU workloads, including machine learning inference, data analysis, and rendering pipelines.
